title: Myagrum coronopus
taxonomy: Myagrum coronopus is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is commonly known as hoary cress or hoop-petticoat. The plant is native to Europe and Asia but has been naturalized in other parts of the world. Myagrum coronopus is often classified within the tribe Brassiceae. However, some sources may group it within the subfamily Brassicoideae.
uses: Myagrum coronopus, also known as Coronopus, is a plant species with a long history of traditional medicinal uses. The plant has been used in various cultures to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, Coronopus is often used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and other skin irritations. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

It is a good source of vitamins A and C, as well as minerals like calcium and iron, which contribute to a balanced diet. side_effects: Myagrum coronopus, also known as Hoary Cress, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
